subject: Payment cache incident — resolution and Redis migration
body:
Summary for leadership visibility ahead of Black Friday:

Issue: load testing surfaced stale pricing on ~8% of checkout requests due to per-pod in-memory caching in payment-service not propagating invalidation across replicas.

Fix: migrated payment-service caching to Redis (already used for sessions), giving a single shared cache all replicas read from. Added p50/p95 latency checks in staging showing ~2ms added latency, well within SLA. Shipped and merged Nov 4, well ahead of the Nov 21 Black Friday freeze window.

No customer-facing impact occurred; this was caught in pre-production load testing.

subject: RE: Oct 2 incident postmortem — billing/notifications cascade
body:
Postmortem summary for the Oct 2 incident (18 min of elevated billing errors, no data loss):

Root cause: billing-service calls notifications-service synchronously with no timeout. When notifications-service degraded due to an unrelated DB issue, billing requests piled up waiting and began timing out themselves.

Fix shipped Oct 5 (PR #315): circuit breaker on the billing->notifications call path, fails open after 5 consecutive failures (skips the notification, logs for async retry, does not block the billing transaction), with a PagerDuty alert on trip so we have visibility when this happens rather than finding out from a support ticket.

Follow-up action: audit other synchronous cross-service calls in the post-monolith-split architecture for similar timeout gaps (tracked in ARCH-20, not yet started).
